questions: Should resizing a video clip have any speed limits (e.g., min 0.25x, max 4x)? Unconstrained speed could produce very slow or fast playback on extreme resizes.

For left-edge resize on video clips: should this also preserve full source and only change speed (pure speed mode), or is the described behavior correct — trim the start and adjust speed for the remaining portion?

When a video clip has already been speed-adjusted via resize, should a subsequent resize compound the speed change (resize from current state) or reset to original source duration? Current plan: compounds from current state.
Should the audio clamp visual cue be a colored border, a snap indicator, or something else? Any existing UX pattern for 'limit reached' in this editor?

success_criteria.criterion: Video clip right-edge resize changes speed, not from/to. Formula: newSpeed = sourceDuration / timelineDuration
Video clip left-edge resize adjusts from and recalculates speed to preserve source end point
Audio clip resize never modifies speed
Audio clip cannot be extended past source asset duration on the timeline
Hold-type clips (images/text) are unaffected by this change
Audio clamping applies during live drag preview (updateResize), not just on end
resolveOverlaps does not corrupt speed/from/to relationship for video clips
rowsToConfig serialization produces correct from/to/speed after speed-based resize
Visual feedback shown when audio clip hits its max resize extent
No new files created — all changes in existing files
Speed changes propagate correctly to VisualClip/AudioTrack playbackRate rendering
Manual smoke tests (8 scenarios in Step 8) pass

assumptions: Compounding speed: subsequent resizes work from the clip's current speed/from/to state, not original source. This is the natural behavior since we save current state at resize start.
No speed limits enforced: the user can make a clip arbitrarily slow or fast via resize. Speed limits can be added later if needed.

Left-edge video resize = trim start + adjust speed: the left edge changes the source trim point (from), then speed adjusts so the remaining source fills the timeline duration. This matches the stated requirement.
Audio left-edge resize: current behavior preserved (adjusts from), no clamping needed since shortening from the left always stays within source duration.
multi-drag does not resize clips: it only repositions, so no speed changes needed there.
The visual cue for audio clamp will be a CSS-based indicator (e.g., a highlighted border or color change on the clip edge) using existing styling patterns.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
